items.question: Which keyword turns a function into a generator?
items.explanation: A function containing yield returns a generator object when called.

items.question: What does an iterator raise when it has no more items?
items.explanation: StopIteration signals exhaustion; for loops catch it to terminate.

items.question: A key advantage of generators over lists is…
options: they can be iterated unlimited times
they are always faster to build fully
items.explanation: Generators produce items lazily, so they don't store the whole sequence.

items.question: What does `yield from sub` do inside a generator?
options: re-yields every item produced by sub
items.answer: re-yields every item produced by sub
items.explanation: yield from delegates iteration to the sub-iterable, yielding all its values.
